scope: blind re-audit of 34 R24-fixed pages + glossary g_453+g_538 (post-iter-7 state)

findings.defect: zh translation for ハウジングサービス is '服务器代管服务' here, but pages 198/561 and glossary g_453 all use '服务器托管服务'. Term should be terminology-stable across the corpus; this inconsistency may confuse learners cross-referencing the glossary.
findings.evidence_excerpt: p200 surface.zh='服务器代管服务'; p198 + p561 + g_453 surface.zh='服务器托管服务'

findings.defect: Redundant parenthetical 'RAT (Remote Access Tool) (Remote Administration Tool / Remote Access Tool) is malware...' — the expansion appears twice because the surface already includes one expansion. Cosmetic; meaning is unambiguous.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.en: 'RAT (Remote Access Tool) (Remote Administration Tool / Remote Access Tool) is malware that...'

findings.defect: Mixed punctuation: zh definition uses ASCII commas ',' instead of full-width '，' in '通过事先备份数据,即使万一计算机内的数据被勒索者扣作人质,也可以从备份中恢复数据.'. Cosmetic; comprehension unaffected.
findings.evidence_excerpt: ...通过事先备份数据,即使万一计算机内的数据被勒索者扣作人质,也可以从备份中恢复数据.

findings.defect: ASCII period '.' used at sentence end inside Chinese text instead of full-width '。' for VPN definition. Cosmetic.
findings.evidence_excerpt: ...在外出地通过互联网安全地访问公司内部网络.

findings.defect: Same ASCII '.' instead of '。' for ペネトレーションテスト definition; cosmetic.
findings.evidence_excerpt: ...来发现安全弱点'的句子出题.

findings.defect: Same ASCII '.' issue; ワンタイムパスワード zh definition.
findings.evidence_excerpt: ...从而防止非法访问的机制'的句子出题.

findings.defect: Chinese definition opens with '风险等级认证' but the surface heading is '基于风险的认证'. The body should reuse the surface term '基于风险的认证' for consistency. Cosmetic terminology drift; meaning intact.
findings.evidence_excerpt: surface.zh='基于风险的认证'; definition.zh begins '风险等级认证是当用户从与平常使用环境不同的环境...'

findings.defect: Definition of ステークホルダ contains an ungrammatical Japanese fragment: 'プロジェクトから受ける外で、必要なスキルを獲得していくこと。' — the second sentence does not belong to a stakeholder definition (it conflates with skill acquisition under resource management). Confuses the learner about what a stakeholder is.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp: 'ステークホルダとは、プロジェクトからの利害や損益を受ける可能性のある人や組織。プロジェクトから受ける外で、必要なスキルを獲得していくこと。'

findings.defect: zh+en mirror the jp defect: 'Beyond the project itself, they also need to acquire the necessary skills.' / '除项目本身外，还需持续获取所需技能。' This is not part of the stakeholder definition and mis-teaches the concept. Should be removed or attached to the correct resource-management entry.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.en: '...individuals or organizations that may gain or lose value from the project. Beyond the project itself, they also need to acquire the necessary skills.'

findings.defect: Awkward phrasing: 'ソフトウェア受入れテストは、システムが正常に稼働することをユーザーが利用者側が確認するテスト。' — jp itself has duplicated agent ('ユーザーが利用者側が'). Cosmetic in zh ('由使用方（用户）确认...') but jp source is ungrammatical.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp: '...システムが正常に稼働することをユーザーが利用者側が確認するテスト。'

findings.defect: Definition of アジャイル開発／反復型開発 contains a contradiction: '一定期間ごとに、ウォーターフォール・モデルなどで、仕様変更に強い。' — agile/iterative is NOT done with the Waterfall model; this sentence is grammatically broken AND technically wrong. The zh and en translations preserved the broken meaning ('These approaches progress in fixed cycles...') but dropped the wrong 'Waterfall' reference, masking the issue. The jp leaf is misleading to native-jp readers.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp: 'アジャイル開発、反復型開発などを覚えておく。一定期間ごとに、ウォーターフォール・モデルなどで、仕様変更に強い。'

findings.defect: プロジェクトマネジメント jp definition is ungrammatical: 'プロジェクトの円滑な作業を制限する。' literally reads 'restricts smooth work'—opposite of intended meaning. zh+en silently corrected to 'support/smooth execution'. Source-jp credibility risk.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp: 'プロジェクトマネジメントとは、プロジェクトの円滑な作業を制限する。WBSとは、プロジェクト全体を階層的に重要な単位に分解したもの。'

findings.defect: RFI/RFP en definition uses 'RFI precedes RFP in the procurement flow' — fine — but jp wording 'RFPと対比してRFIの先に出る用語' is awkward. Acceptable; no semantic loss.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp: '...RFPと対比してRFIの先に出る用語である。'

findings.defect: Surface for 組込みソフトウェア includes '（固件）' (firmware) parenthetical in zh but not in jp/en. Adds extra concept not in source. Minor scope creep.
findings.evidence_excerpt: surface.jp='組込みソフトウェア'; surface.zh='嵌入式软件（固件）'; surface.en='Embedded Software (Firmware)'

findings.defect: Surface for ハウジングサービス here uses 'Server Housing Service' in en, but pages 198 and 200 use 'Housing Service' in en and glossary g_453 uses 'Housing Service'. Terminology inconsistency across corpus.
findings.evidence_excerpt: p561 surface.en='Server Housing Service'; p198+p200+g_453 surface.en='Housing Service'

findings.defect: クラウドコンピューティング defined as 'A service that uses servers...(location is unclear)' — this is a textbook-style cheat sheet mnemonic contrasting Hosting Service (location clear) vs Cloud (location unclear). Faithful to source convention; not a defect, but flag for educators: real-world definition is broader. Acceptable as exam-textbook content.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.en: 'A service that uses servers located in and owned by another company (the location of the contracted server is unclear)'

findings.defect: ランサムウェア対策 jp definition reduces countermeasure to ONLY backup: 'ランサムウェアへの対応策は「データを外部記憶装置にバックアップすること」です。' — In modern IT-Passport content, ransomware countermeasures also include offline/immutable backup, network isolation, EDR, and patching. As a single-bullet IT-Passport-style answer it is acceptable, but flagging as WARN because it does not explicitly mention 'offline / disconnected' backup (a connected backup can also be encrypted by ransomware). May produce incorrect exam study habit.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp: 'ランサムウェアへの対応策は「データを外部記憶装置にバックアップすること」です。データをバックアップしておくことで、万が一、コンピュータ内のデータを人質に取られたとしても、バックアップからデータを復元できます。'

findings.defect: Definition zh for 組込みソフトウェア is '为实现特定功能的软件' — missing predicate verb. Should be '是为实现特定功能的软件' or '指为实现特定功能而设计的软件'. Cosmetic; comprehension unaffected.
findings.evidence_excerpt: definition.jp='特定の機能を実現するためのソフトウェアである'; definition.zh='为实现特定功能的软件'

findings.defect: 貸借対照表 jp definition is terse ('会社の全財産が記載された表。') but zh+en have been expanded into a fuller accounting-equation definition. This is a >50% rewrite/expansion versus source. Although the expansion is technically correct (assets = liabilities + equity), it crosses Rule A 50% rewrite threshold and the trilingual leaves diverge in scope. Flag for compression-audit consistency.
findings.evidence_excerpt: jp='会社の全財産が記載された表。' (15 chars); en='A financial statement that lists a company's assets, liabilities, and equity at a specific point in time, satisfying the fundamental accounting equation: assets = liabilities + equity.' (~210 chars)

findings.defect: Page 561 is a chapter glossary list (76 leaves) with many ultra-short jp definitions paired to longer zh/en. Pattern is consistent and acceptable for a recap page; no FAIL but the file is dense.
findings.evidence_excerpt: 76 leaves, all <=3-line definitions

findings.defect: Page 562 mixes Chapter 6/7/8 review entries with section_path tags ['6章']/['7章']/['8章']. Structure clean; no defect. Note that the source text appears compressed (many entries are aggregator-style two-sentence summaries) which is consistent with a chapter-review page.
findings.evidence_excerpt: 31 leaves across 3 chapters

glossary_findings.defect: g_453 is internally consistent (jp=ハウジングサービス, zh=服务器托管服务, en=Housing Service, reading=haujingu sābisu). However, p561 uses surface.en='Server Housing Service' which deviates from this glossary canonical form. Recommend aligning p561 to glossary canonical 'Housing Service'.
glossary_findings.evidence_excerpt: g_453.surface.en='Housing Service'; p561 surface.en='Server Housing Service'

glossary_findings.defect: g_538 internally consistent (jp=ホスティングサービス, zh=主机租用服务, en=Hosting Service, reading=hosutingu sābisu). occurrences=[198,200,561] match findings. No defect.
glossary_findings.evidence_excerpt: g_538.surface={'jp':'ホスティングサービス','zh':'主机租用服务','en':'Hosting Service'}

perspective_pass_summary.IT_pro: PASS with 2 WARN (p539 ransomware single-vector, p562 アジャイル jp contradiction) and 1 INFO (p200 cloud textbook-mnemonic definition).
perspective_pass_summary.educator: PASS with 2 WARN (p200 zh terminology drift for ハウジングサービス, p562 ステークホルダ definition mis-merged with skill acquisition) and 2 INFO.
perspective_pass_summary.reader: PASS with 0 WARN and 8 INFO (mostly ASCII punctuation in zh, terminology drift, redundant parenthetical).

summary.release_verdict: PASS — zero FAIL/release-impacting defects on the 34 R24-fixed pages. The 5 WARN items are Phase-2 polish (terminology consistency for ハウジングサービス zh/en, p562 ステークホルダ + アジャイル jp source-text quality, p539 ransomware countermeasure narrow scope). The 16 INFO items are cosmetic (punctuation, redundant parentheticals, minor phrasing). Glossary g_453 and g_538 are internally consistent.
summary.rule_d_compliance: PASS — only post-fix output files were read; no iter-3/4/5/6 audits, no r23/r24 logs, no scripts, no ADRs from blind list were touched.
